name: FC Metall Nord
philosophy: Verticality over possession. Press from the front. Win the ball in the opponent's half. Quick transitions. Flank overload with compact rest-defence.

terminology.pressing_trigger: Ball-side winger collapses on the CB when he receives facing his own goal — that's our green light.
terminology.rest_defence: Back four stays compact, 18-20m between lines. No chasing shadows.
terminology.verticality: Don't go sideways. If the 6 receives between lines, the first look is forward to the 10 or into the channel for the 9.
terminology.flank_overload: Right winger pins the FB, 10 drifts wide to create 2v1, RB underlaps. Left side stays narrow as an outlet.
terminology.compact_block: When we lose it, everyone within 30m of the ball. No one jogs back — sprint to position.
terminology.first_touch_pressure: The moment their GK has it, the 9 cuts the passing lane to the CB. We don't let them breathe.
terminology.channel_run: 9 peels into the channel between CB and FB — drags a marker, opens the pocket for the 10.
terminology.switch_isolation: If the left side gets isolated 1v1, the 7 takes him on. No double-team — trust your man.
terminology.build_patience: If they sit in a mid-block, don't force it. Circulate at the back, wait for their 6 to step, then play through.
terminology.transition_lock: When we win it back in their half, two touches max. Shot or cross. No recycling.

principles: Press from the front — the 9 is the first defender
Verticality: forward first, sideways only as a last resort
Compact rest-defence: never more than 20m between lines
Flank overload: create 2v1s on the ball side
Transition speed: win it, two touches, shot or cross
Trust the 1v1 on the weak side — don't overcommit
The 6 is the metronome — always available, always forward-thinking
GK plays as a sweeper-keeper, high line is non-negotiable

players.name: Lukas Brandt
players.profile: Sweeper-keeper, good with feet, commands the high line. Distribution: 70% short to CBs, 30% long to channels.

players.name: Marco Heil
players.profile: Attacking fullback, underlaps well, recovery pace. Tends to get caught high on counters.

players.name: Stefan Kross
players.profile: Left-footed, aggressive in the air, leader. Steps into midfield when their 6 drops.

players.name: Daniel Vogt
players.profile: Right-footed, composed, reads the game. Stays as cover when Kross steps.

players.name: Philipp Auer
players.profile: Defensive-minded, stays compact, rarely overlaps. Good 1v1 defender.

players.name: Jonas Riedel
players.profile: Metronome, always available, forward-thinking. Shield for the back four. Weakness: can be pressed into errors.

players.name: Erik Schäfer
players.profile: Box-to-box, late runs into the box, pressing engine. Tires after 70 min.

players.name: Niklas Wendt
players.profile: Creative, drifts wide to create overloads, vision. Not a defender — needs cover.

players.name: Tomas Krüger
players.profile: Pacey, pins the FB, cuts inside to shoot. Right foot dominant. Pressing trigger player.

players.name: Felix Hartmann
players.profile: Channel runner, holds up play, presses the CB. Left foot. Link-up with the 10 is key.

players.name: Andreas Loth
players.profile: Isolation specialist, 1v1 threat, left foot. Stays wide. Weakness: tracks back late.
